title: Complete the PyO3-to-IPC cutover and delete the PyO3 transport
description: THE keystone. Both transports are fully built and the App is transport-agnostic (Box<dyn BridgeService>), but Cargo.toml:15 still ships default=["pyo3-bridge"], so a flagless cargo build runs the deprecated in-process PyO3 path (src/main.rs:360-368). This blocks decoupled packaging (ADR-017), makes crystalmath-server the sole Python entrypoint (required by ADR-014/015/016/023), and removes the PYO3_PYTHON build dance. Flip the default to IPC, delete the pyo3-gated code in src/bridge.rs (init_python_backend, bridge_worker_loop, dispatch_rpc, BridgeHandle, ~26 PyO3 sites) and configure_python_env in main.rs, remove the optional pyo3 dep (Cargo.toml:32), then unify the two Python JSON-RPC dispatch registries (server/_handlers.py HANDLER_REGISTRY vs api.py CrystalController._rpc_registry) into one domain.verb table and fix the init-error-masked-as-Method-not-found bug. Realizes ADR-014; finishes ADR-006's keystone.

ADRs realized: ADR-014, ADR-006, ADR-003
Depends on epics: none
Folds in existing issues (reparented as children): crystalmath-bc6, crystalmath-dew, crystalmath-gn8

  2. [P0] Delete src/bridge.rs PyO3 internals and configure_python_env — Remove init_python_backend, bridge_worker_loop, dispatch_rpc, BridgeHandle and the ~26 PyO3 call sites plus the main.rs PYO3_PYTHON build dance.
  3. [P1] Wire or gate the 9 IPC-build dead-code warnings — Resolve the dead-code warnings on the now-default IPC build (e.g. ipc/client.rs:480 set_timeout) so the default build is warning-clean.
  4. [P1] Unify the two JSON-RPC dispatch registries into one domain.verb table — Collapse server/_handlers.py HANDLER_REGISTRY and api.py CrystalController._rpc_registry into a single dispatch surface.
  5. [P1] Surface controller/DB init failures instead of masking as Method-not-found or demo-mode — Fix the init-error masking (bridge.rs find_database_path demo-mode return None; server returning METHOD_NOT_FOUND when the controller failed to initialize).
  6. [P2] Make the IPC build the default CI gate — Update CI so the flagless build + ipc_integration tests are the gating job; remove the PyO3 build matrix.
acceptance_criteria:
- A flagless `cargo build` / `./scripts/build-tui.sh` produces a binary that talks ONLY to crystalmath-server over stdio JSON-RPC; the pyo3 dependency and pyo3-bridge feature are gone from Cargo.toml.
- src/bridge.rs PyO3 internals and configure_python_env are deleted; the IPC client (src/ipc/client.rs, src/bridge_ipc.rs) is the only live transport with no dead-code warnings.
- A single domain.verb dispatch table serves all RPCs; controller/DB init failures surface as honest errors (not METHOD_NOT_FOUND or silent demo-mode).
- IPC integration tests (tests/ipc_integration.rs, tests/ipc_bridge_integration.rs) pass in CI as the default build.

title: [remove-hardcoded-ssh-password] Remove committed root/adminadmin SSH credentials from cluster config
description:
python/crystalmath/high_level/clusters.py hardcodes ssh_user="root", ssh_password="adminadmin" for vasp-01/02/03 (lines 257-258, 275-276, 293-294) in the module-level BEEFCAKE2_NODES dict. The value is actively used: interpolated into `sshpass -p '...'` (line 154, exposing it in the process table) and passed to AiiDA computer configuration with look_for_keys disabled (lines 1468-1470). docs/workflows/cluster-setup.md reproduces the password as the canonical credential. No env-var/keyring/vault indirection exists. Replace with SSH key auth (ssh_password=None + key path from env/secret store) or at minimum os.getenv('VASP_NODE_SSH_PASSWORD'); drop the ssh_user='root' default; switch get_ssh_connection_string to `sshpass -e` if sshpass is retained; scrub the password from docs and git history.

AREA: Security
EFFORT: M | BLOCKER: True
KEY FILES: python/crystalmath/high_level/clusters.py, docs/workflows/cluster-setup.md, tui/config/clusters/beefcake.yaml
DEPENDS ON (slugs): none
acceptance_criteria: No literal password remains in source or docs; grep for 'adminadmin' across the repo returns zero matches; credentials are sourced from env/secret store at runtime; VASP nodes default to key auth; secret rotated.
close_reason: Credentials now sourced from VASP_NODE_SSH_USER/VASP_NODE_SSH_PASSWORD env; default key auth; sshpass -e (no process-table exposure); docs/yaml scrubbed. Repo grep for 'adminadmin' is clean. Secret still needs out-of-band rotation + history purge.

title: [fix-pytest-collection-aiosqlite] Unbreak pytest collection (aiosqlite import in deprecated tui/ aborts the whole suite)
description:
Root testpaths includes tui/tests (pyproject.toml:98). tui/tests/test_materials_api.py imports the materials_api package whose __init__.py (tui/src/core/materials_api/__init__.py:33) unconditionally re-exports from cache.py, which does a bare top-level `import aiosqlite` (tui/src/core/materials_api/cache.py:29). aiosqlite is only in the crystal-tui[materials] optional extra, absent from a default `uv sync`, so `uv run pytest` halts with ModuleNotFoundError at collection BEFORE any python/tests core test runs. This zeroes out all CI/test signal for the Python core (the ADR-006 SSOT). Fix (ADR-006-aligned): remove tui/tests from root testpaths (tui/ is deprecated and already has its own testpaths), and/or guard the import in cache.py with try/except ImportError. Update pre-push hook (scripts/pre-push-quality.sh:111) to stop running tui/tests.

AREA: Test suite / CI health
EFFORT: S | BLOCKER: True
KEY FILES: pyproject.toml, tui/src/core/materials_api/cache.py, tui/src/core/materials_api/__init__.py, tui/pyproject.toml, scripts/pre-push-quality.sh
DEPENDS ON (slugs): none
acceptance_criteria: `uv run pytest` from repo root collects and runs the python/tests suite to completion with zero collection errors; pre-push hook no longer fails on a default `uv sync`. python/tests result count is non-zero and reported.
close_reason: Scoped root testpaths + quality gates to the python/ core; guarded optional aiosqlite/ase imports; formatted core. uv run pytest python/tests/ now runs to completion: 476 passed, 41 skipped (was: collection aborted).

Note: protocols.get_runner('jobflow') at protocols.py:761 already returns a real Atomate2Bridge, so the bridge is the seam to finish, not recreate. Realizes ADR-011/013/024.

title: Introduce versioned emmet-style pydantic TaskDocuments with provenance
description: The linchpin of the science chain. Today the result schema is an untyped blob (models.py:249 key_results: dict[str,Any]|None) and six competing JobState/JobStatus enums coexist. There is no emmet TaskDocument, no calc_inputs/outputs sections, no versioning, no input_hash/provenance (those words appear only in jobflow_store.py docstrings). Add emmet-core as a dependency and define versioned per-code pydantic TaskDocuments carrying first-class provenance (input_hash, code+version, structure uuid, parent-job uuids, content-addressed raw_paths). Every store, cache, handoff edge, and registry references this document's hash+lineage fields, so it must land before the store/IR/handoff epics. Realizes ADR-009.

title: Generate Rust serde types from pydantic models to kill wire drift
description: Two hand-synced copies of the wire contract exist: src/models.rs (~30 structs/enums, header literally says 'These Rust structs match the Pydantic models in models.py') and python/crystalmath/models.py. Every field add edits both by hand; a rename breaks a TUI tab at runtime, not cargo build at CI. There is no build.rs, no typeshare/schemars/datamodel-codegen, no JSON Schema export. Make pydantic the source of truth: export JSON Schema from models.py and GENERATE src/models.rs in build.rs so drift becomes a compile error. Per the completeness critique this is drift-PREVENTION and must land EARLY (right after the IPC cutover makes the wire real), BEFORE the data-model churn of ADR-009 — otherwise every TaskDocument field gets hand-mirrored. Realizes ADR-016.

  4. [P3] Document the no-drift contract in AGENTS.md — Record that models.py is the single source of truth and src/models.rs is generated; update bd-9yp-flagged docs.

title: [aiida-launcher-import-fix] Fix AiiDA launcher imports broken by sys.path insertion / wrong namespace
description:
launch_geometry_optimization and launch_band_structure in python/crystalmath/workflows/aiida_launcher.py always fail. The module inserts tui/src at sys.path[0] (lines 27-30), which makes `from aiida import orm` (line 174) resolve to the local tui/src/aiida package that does not export orm — so the try block fails at line 174 before reaching the workchain import at 176/271. If aiida-core is pre-imported elsewhere the failure flips to the workchain line instead. Either way both functions return success=False. Verification confirmed P1 but corrected the mechanism (sys.path shadowing, not just a wrong workchain namespace). Fix the whole try block: import aiida-core's orm/engine from the installed package and import the Crystal workchains from their actual tui path (or migrate them into crystalmath) without shadowing the aiida namespace.

title: [guard-silent-simulated-stub-results] Stop silently returning {simulated:True} success when no runner is configured
description:
BaseAnalysisRunner._execute_step (python/crystalmath/high_level/runners.py:1177-1187) returns StepResult(success=True, outputs={'simulated': True}) with only a warning when self._runner is None. All four runner subclasses (StandardAnalysis, PhononAnalysis, ElasticAnalysis, TransportAnalysis) inherit this. _create_slurm_runner (379-411) returns None on any import error (including the deprecated-tui import failure), so a user passing cluster=beefcake2 can silently land in stub mode. Over IPC these fake-success results are indistinguishable from real ones to the Rust TUI. The AGENTS.md allow_stub_execution gate exists in the deprecated tui/src/core/workflow.py:1085-1091 but was never ported to the Python-core runners (verification confirmed the gate exists in the engine, so the broader 'gate entirely absent' claim was refuted — the real gap is the unguarded simulated path in high_level/runners). Fix: require metadata['allow_stub_execution']=True (per AGENTS.md) before returning a simulated result, otherwise raise a distinct error.

Result: quacc recipes execute synchronously in-process (the server thread) rather than dispatching to SLURM — the 'future' returned is a plain dict, and get_status/get_result hedge for that. This is the live jobs.submit path via server/handlers/jobs.py. Add to_parsl_config() building a parsl.Config with HighThroughputExecutor+SlurmProvider, call parsl.load() in ParslRunner.__init__, and route cluster_name to the right executor label so non-local engines dispatch to SLURM.

title: [multistep-workflow-execution] Implement multi-step workflow execution: dependency chaining + wait-for-completion + inter-step data handoff
description:
Multi-step scientific workflows (relax->SCF->bands/DOS, GW->BSE, phonon, VASP->YAMBO) are non-functional in the production runner path. Three linked defects: (1) BaseAnalysisRunner._execute_workflow (python/crystalmath/high_level/runners.py:1118-1159) submits steps sequentially but submit_async (slurm_runner.py:395-450) returns immediately after submission without polling, so step N+1 fires while step N is still PENDING/RUNNING; (2) step.outputs_to_pass is declared at 6 sites (runners.py:1533/1551/1706/1724/2320/2334) but _execute_step (1161-1214) never reads it or transfers WAVECAR/CHGCAR/ndb.* between steps; (3) SLURM dependency chaining (--dependency=afterok) is not implemented (submit_composite_async at slurm_runner.py:1191-1222 stashes pending_steps in metadata that nothing ever reads). Implement either real SLURM afterok dependency chaining or a poll-until-complete loop that stages outputs_to_pass files into the next step's work_dir before submitting it. (Note: the atomate2 submit_composite/MultiCodeFlowBuilder stubs are a separate Phase-3 path, not the blocker — verification downgraded those to P2/P3.)

title: [generate-vasp-potcar] Generate POTCAR for VASP jobs instead of a placeholder
description:
_generate_vasp_inputs writes a 'POTCAR_NEEDED' sentinel (python/crystalmath/integrations/slurm_runner.py:780-784) and the SLURM script calls a hardcoded /opt/vasp/scripts/generate_potcar.sh with zero args and no error handling (946-949); _generate_slurm_script has no structure/element info to pass. VASP aborts immediately on a missing POTCAR, so every VASP job fails. Working pieces exist but are unwired: python/crystalmath/vasp/generator.py:211-218 derives potcar_symbols from structure, and crystalmath/quacc/potcar.py has validate_potcars/get_potcar_path. Wire pymatgen Potcar generation (from VASP_PP_PATH) into the submission path to write a real POTCAR alongside INCAR/POSCAR/KPOINTS, or pass the element list to the cluster script with hard error handling.

title: [persist-slurm-job-state] Persist SLURM job state so it survives server restart
description:
SLURMWorkflowRunner stores submitted jobs only in self._jobs (python/crystalmath/integrations/slurm_runner.py:267) with no persistence. After an IPC-server restart, get_status (1246), get_result (1315) and cancel (1525) return failed/False for every previously-submitted workflow_id — live cluster jobs are silently orphaned from the tracking layer (the SLURM jobs keep running, but the user can no longer poll/cancel them). title: [editor-open-save-files] Editor tab: add file open (Ctrl+O) and save (Ctrl+S)
description:
The Editor tab handler (src/main.rs:805-816) supports only Ctrl+Enter submit and raw key forwarding. There is no way to open an existing .d12/VASP file from disk (open_file at app.rs:3465 is #[allow(dead_code)], called only from Materials/D12 generation paths) and no save path at all (editor_content at app.rs:3565 is also dead-code-annotated 'Planned for file save feature'). Computational chemists routinely have pre-existing input files; an editor that can neither open nor persist them is not production-usable. Add Ctrl+O (path prompt -> read -> app.open_file) and Ctrl+S (write editor lines to editor_file_path, surface errors via set_error).

title: [ci-run-rust-tests-and-ipc-build] Run cargo test and the IPC (--no-default-features) build automatically in CI
description:
ci-rust.yml gates rust-tests behind workflow_dispatch only, so cargo test (including 40+ unit tests in src/app_tests.rs and the IPC integration tests) never runs on PR/push. The rust-quality job runs clippy with default (pyo3-bridge) features only — the IPC transport (src/ipc/*, src/bridge_ipc.rs, the cfg(not(pyo3-bridge)) main.rs branch) is never built or linted in automated CI, so IPC regressions go undetected. The default-feature clippy also has an implicit python3-dev header dependency with no setup step. Add automatic steps: `cargo test --no-default-features` and `cargo build --no-default-features` + `cargo clippy --no-default-features -- -D warnings` on src/** PRs. Integration tests already honor CRYSTALMATH_SKIP_IPC_TESTS for environments without a live server.

description:
CrystalController and the SQLite backend lazily import from the deprecated tui/src package across 18+ sites and 9+ methods: api.py:170 `from src.core.database import Database`; materials_api service at 991/1037/1075; connection_manager + slurm_runner at 1127/1215/1379/1490/1777; templates at 2167/2228; backends/sqlite.py:57-63 injects tui/src on sys.path. The high_level/slurm_runner also does a sys.path hack (slurm_runner.py:528-534). These work today only because crystal-tui is editable-installed in the workspace .venv; crystalmath-server cannot be installed/distributed standalone (the ADR-006 target) and these methods silently return IMPORT_ERROR/NO_DATABASE JSON when crystal-tui is absent. Migrate Database, ConnectionManager, MaterialsService, TemplateManager, and the SLURM runner into python/crystalmath/ (e.g. crystalmath.db, crystalmath.storage) and rewrite the imports to the canonical package path. This also removes the runtime coupling that blocks the IPC default-flip and is needed for the standalone install story.

title: [validate-recipe-import-allowlist] Allowlist recipe string before __import__ in jobs.submit IPC handler
description:
python/crystalmath/server/handlers/jobs.py:110 takes user-supplied `recipe` from the IPC request and checks only truthiness, then passes it unchanged to runner.submit(recipe_fullname=recipe) which calls __import__(module_path, fromlist=[func_name]) in python/crystalmath/quacc/runner.py:134. Any IPC client (any local process running as the same user — socket is chmod 0600, which scopes but does not eliminate the risk) can cause arbitrary module import / import-time side-effect execution. Verification corrected severity from P0 to P1 because the 0600 socket blocks remote-unauthenticated RCE. Fix: validate recipe against discover_vasp_recipes() output or assert recipe.startswith('quacc.recipes.') in handle_jobs_submit, and add the same defense-in-depth check in _import_recipe (runner.py:116).

AREA: Security
EFFORT: S | BLOCKER: True
KEY FILES: python/crystalmath/server/handlers/jobs.py, python/crystalmath/quacc/runner.py, python/crystalmath/quacc/discovery.py
DEPENDS ON (slugs): none
acceptance_criteria: jobs.submit rejects any recipe not in the discovered allowlist (or not under an approved prefix) with a structured error; a unit test asserts a malicious recipe like 'os.system' is refused; _import_recipe enforces the same constraint.
close_reason: is_allowed_recipe() allowlist enforced in JobRunner._import_recipe (all runners) + early rejection in jobs.submit handler. Restricts dynamic import to quacc.recipes.* dotted-identifier paths. 4 new security tests; 480 passed.

title: Adopt pydantic-settings as the single config resolver across TUI, CLI, and core
description: Config is fragmented: quacc/config.py uses pydantic BaseModel + manual json.dump; high_level/clusters.py uses plain @dataclass; backends/__init__.py and server/__init__.py hand-roll platform path resolution (find_database_path, get_default_socket_path) mirroring Rust dirs::, which is the root of the socket-path mismatch. pydantic-settings is not a dependency. Collapse all config resolution to one pydantic-settings resolver in the core (layered XDG-TOML + project-TOML + env, per-code sections, documented precedence env>project>user>defaults); the Rust TUI and Bash CLI read RESOLVED values via the stdio handshake / config.get / a --export-bash shim and never parse TOML independently. Cheap first step: consolidate the duplicated path-resolution helpers. Realizes ADR-015 (supersedes ADR-005).
design:
ADRs realized: ADR-015, ADR-005
Depends on epics: ipc-cutover-delete-pyo3
Folds in existing issues (reparented as children): crystalmath-9yp

  2. [P2] Consolidate duplicated path-resolution helpers — Merge find_database_path (backends/__init__.py) and get_default_socket_path (server/__init__.py) into the resolver.
  3. [P3] Expose config.get over JSON-RPC and a --export-bash shim — TUI and CLI read resolved values via the handshake/config.get and the bash shim; remove independent TOML parsing.
  4. [P3] Migrate quacc/config.py and high_level/clusters.py onto the resolver — Replace the ad-hoc BaseModel/json.dump and @dataclass cluster configs with the unified settings model.
acceptance_criteria:
- pydantic-settings is a dependency; one CrystalMathConfig resolver with documented precedence (env>project>user>defaults) and per-code sections is the sole config source in the core.
- The Rust TUI and Bash CLI obtain resolved values via config.get / --export-bash; no component parses TOML independently, structurally ending the socket-path mismatch.
- The duplicated find_database_path / get_default_socket_path helpers are consolidated into the resolver.

title: Replace bespoke ADAPTIVE recovery with custodian-style per-code error handlers
description: Today recovery is a bespoke design only: protocols.py ErrorRecoveryStrategy.ADAPTIVE (l.121) + an unimplemented ErrorHandler Protocol, plus a fork-vendored VASPErrorHandler (_vendor/runners/vasp_errors.py) used as a fallback in server/handlers/jobs.py:596 with an inline regex _analyze_vasp_errors_fallback. custodian is not a dependency. Adopt custodian's wrap-detect-patch-restart loop beneath the jobflow engine: the maintained VaspErrorHandler/QE catalogue plus thin CRYSTAL23/YAMBO ErrorHandler subclasses CrystalMath owns; graph-level recovery via jobflow Response. Delete the bespoke ADAPTIVE machinery, substring-grep _is_retryable_error, and the vendored vasp_errors patterns. Realizes ADR-018.

title: Adopt jobflow-remote as the one HPC execution backend; demote bespoke SLURM/SSH, AiiDA opt-in

title: Extract crystalmath/decks/ deep input-deck generator from SLURMWorkflowRunner
description: Deepening from a /improve-codebase-architecture grilling (design in CONTEXT.md). The SLURM runner (~2000 lines) fuses DFT input-deck generation with SSH transport/scripting/persistence, and reimplements VASP generation inline (duplicating crystalmath/vasp/generator.py). Extract a deep seam: crystalmath/decks/ with a CodeDeckGenerator interface (generate(structure, workflow_type, parameters) -> InputDeck), per-code adapters (Vasp absorbs vasp/generator.py, Crystal wraps the vendored crystal_d12, Qe, Yambo) looked up via a registry, an InputDeck content value object (file contents as strings + POTCAR symbols + metadata, no I/O), and a separate stage(deck, work_dir) that writes files and assembles POTCAR from VASP_PP_PATH (fail-fast — the d4l behavior moves here). SLURMWorkflowRunner shrinks to generate->stage->submit/poll/retrieve. Enables a LocalWorkflowRunner (generate->stage->subprocess) as a 2nd WorkflowRunner adapter. Relates to drm, d4l, and candidate 4.
acceptance_criteria: crystalmath/decks/ exists with CodeDeckGenerator + registry + InputDeck + stage(); VaspDeckGenerator/CrystalDeckGenerator/QeDeckGenerator/YamboDeckGenerator produce correct InputDeck content asserted THROUGH the generator interface (no work_dir, no cluster); stage() owns file writing + POTCAR fail-fast with its own tests; vasp/generator.py is absorbed (one VASP generator); SLURMWorkflowRunner delegates to decks and shrinks; suite green.
close_reason: Extracted crystalmath/decks: InputDeck + CodeDeckGenerator seam + registry + 4 per-code adapters (Vasp/Crystal/Qe/Yambo) + stage() with POTCAR fail-fast. SLURMWorkflowRunner delegates (generate->stage), inline generators deleted (-452 lines, 2004->1552); duplicated VASP generation removed (adapter wraps vasp/generator.py). Done TDD in 5 vertical slices; deck-content tests now assert through the interface. 518 passed default; no regressions.

title: [pre-existing] pymatgen-version test failures in test_pymatgen_bridge + test_vasp_generator
description: With pymatgen installed, 5 tests fail on clean HEAD (independent of crystalmath-z46/d4l/drm/b0d): test_pymatgen_bridge.py::test_real_symmetry_analysis, ::test_full_workflow_cif_to_validation; test_vasp_generator.py::test_generate_complete_inputs (+2). They are masked in CI because the default uv sync does not install pymatgen, so they are importorskip-skipped. Surfaced while running the scientific-execution suite with 'uv run --with pymatgen'. Likely a pymatgen-API/version drift. Fix the assertions/usage to match the pinned pymatgen, and have CI run a pymatgen-extra job so these don't silently rot.

title: [implement-highlevel-api-or-mark-stub] Implement (or clearly defer) HighThroughput/WorkflowBuilder execution methods
description:
The high-level API is part-stub. run_standard_analysis and from_mp work in mock/local mode (verification refuted the 'entirely stub' claim), but Workflow.run/run_async/submit/get_status/get_result/cancel all raise NotImplementedError (builder.py:215-328), from_poscar/from_structure/from_aiida raise NotImplementedError (api.py:364/401/436), and run_standard_analysis uses the complete_workflow() testing hook to auto-complete in mock mode with no real cluster polling (api.py:265-267); the cluster= param (api.py:156) is silently ignored. from_structure/from_poscar are one-line delegations to run_standard_analysis (the _load_structure helper at api.py:444-488 already handles paths and Structures). Either implement Workflow.run by delegating to the runner infrastructure and wire real polling, or remove the unimplemented surface from __all__ and document it as deferred so callers do not hit confusing NotImplementedErrors.

AREA: High-level API
EFFORT: XL | BLOCKER: False
KEY FILES: python/crystalmath/high_level/api.py, python/crystalmath/high_level/builder.py, python/crystalmath/high_level/runners.py
DEPENDS ON (slugs): multistep-workflow-execution, parsl-runner-configure-executor
acceptance_criteria: Either Workflow.run / from_poscar / from_structure execute a real (or properly mocked) workflow with cluster routing honored, or the unimplemented methods are removed from the public API and documented as deferred; no advertised public method raises a bare NotImplementedError at runtime.

ci-cli.yml:30 runs `bats cli/tests/integration/*.bats` with continue-on-error: true, so broken end-to-end CLI tests do not block merges even though the CLI is marked Production in AGENTS.md. The integration tests use mock binaries (cli/tests/mocks/crystalOMP), so there is no inherent CI limitation. Remove continue-on-error, or scope it only to tests that genuinely need real CRYSTAL23 binaries (guarded and skipped in CI).

DEPENDS ON (slugs): none
acceptance_criteria: CLI integration test failures block the PR; any test requiring real binaries is explicitly skipped in CI rather than globally advisory.
notes: INVESTIGATED, NOT hard-gated yet. Running 'bats cli/tests/integration/*.bats' locally (macOS) shows MANY failures (tests 2,4,16-22,44,52,... — explain-mode, no-gum, output-retrieval). These are very likely macOS-specific (BSD vs GNU coreutils, bash 3.2, missing gum/timeout), but I cannot verify the Linux CI result from here. Removing continue-on-error blindly would risk blocking ALL cli PRs. NEXT: run the integration suite on ubuntu-latest CI (or a Linux container), triage real failures, skip tests needing real CRYSTAL23 binaries, THEN remove continue-on-error. ci-cli.yml left unchanged.

There is no release artifact: build-tui.sh requires an in-tree .venv and PYO3_PYTHON (scripts/build-tui.sh:14-19), the default Cargo feature is pyo3-bridge, and there is no release workflow, pre-built binary, Homebrew formula, or wheel. The getting-started docs still point users at the deprecated Textual TUI. ADR-006 explicitly notes a standalone binary becomes possible once PyO3 is gone. After the IPC default-flip, add a GitHub Actions release workflow building darwin-aarch64 + linux-x86_64 binaries, publish the crystalmath Python package, provide a one-line install, and update tui-install.md / installation.md to the Rust TUI.

title: [aiida-backend-detect-dft-code] Detect actual DFT code in AiiDA backend instead of hardcoding CRYSTAL
description:
AiiDABackend hardcodes dft_code=DftCode.CRYSTAL (backends/aiida.py:112) and the same in api.py:1996; get_job_details omits dft_code entirely and falls back to the CRYSTAL default. The QueryBuilder projection does not even request process_type/process_label. So VASP/QE jobs display as 'crystal'. The DftCode enum (models.py:91-100) also lacks yambo/phonopy. Verification downgraded P1->P2 (display/monitoring correctness only; no routing branches on it, no data corruption). Inspect node.process_type/process_label and map to the correct DftCode; extend the enum as needed for codes managed via AiiDA.

AREA: DFT backends / monitoring
EFFORT: S | BLOCKER: False
KEY FILES: python/crystalmath/backends/aiida.py, python/crystalmath/api.py, python/crystalmath/models.py
DEPENDS ON (slugs): none
acceptance_criteria: A VASP or QE AiiDA job reports its true dft_code in JobStatus/JobDetails; a test covers the mapping; the enum covers all AiiDA-managed codes.

title: [fix-phonon-workflow-rpc-handler] Fix create_phonon_workflow_json runtime bugs (wrong names/methods crash the handler)
description:
create_phonon_workflow_json (python/crystalmath/api.py) crashes before returning due to multiple mismatches against phonon.py: imports DFTCode but the class is PhononDFTCode (api.py:2527 vs phonon.py:26); constructs PhononMethod('finite_displacement') which is not a valid member (phonon.py:18-23); passes symmetry= but the field is use_symmetry= (phonon.py:59); calls workflow.generate_displacements(structure) which does not exist (only generate_displacements_phonopy); references result.num_displacements vs n_displacements; and returns key 'displacements' not 'inputs' so the Rust job-submission loop (app.rs:1412) finds no jobs. (The convergence-study path is correctly wired end-to-end — that part of the broader 'no execution glue' claim was refuted.) Fix the import/method/field names and return an 'inputs' array consistent with the Rust submit loop.

AREA: Workflow engine
EFFORT: S | BLOCKER: False
KEY FILES: python/crystalmath/api.py, python/crystalmath/workflows/phonon.py
DEPENDS ON (slugs): none
acceptance_criteria: create_phonon_workflow_json returns a valid response with an 'inputs' array; the Rust side submits the phonon displacement jobs; covered by a Python test on the handler.
close_reason: Fixed all 6 runtime crashes (PhononDFTCode, method default, use_symmetry, dropped acoustic_sum_rule, generate_displacements_phonopy, n_displacements) and return key -> 'inputs' with name+content. 3 new tests; 489 passed.

title: [python-db-path-resolution-cli] Mirror Rust find_database_path in Python so the CLI/server open the same DB
description:
DB path resolution lives only in Rust (src/bridge.rs:454-563: CRYSTAL_TUI_DB env, project-root, CWD, XDG, platform data dir). The IPC server is covered because the Rust client injects CRYSTAL_TUI_DB into the spawned server (src/ipc/client.rs:230-232). But the standalone Python CLI (python/crystalmath/cli.py:37-39) has no env-var resolution and defaults to None, which falls through to DemoBackend (backends/__init__.py:180-193) — a user running `crystal list` without CRYSTAL_TUI_DB silently operates against demo data. Verification downgraded P1->P2 (primary Rust+IPC path is correct; CLI is secondary; wrong data not crash). Implement a Python find_database_path mirroring the Rust chain and call it as the fallback in CrystalController.__init__ and the CLI.

AREA: Config / packaging
EFFORT: M | BLOCKER: False
KEY FILES: python/crystalmath/cli.py, python/crystalmath/api.py, python/crystalmath/backends/__init__.py, src/bridge.rs
DEPENDS ON (slugs): none
acceptance_criteria: Running the Python CLI without CRYSTAL_TUI_DB resolves the same database the Rust TUI/server uses (no silent DemoBackend); a test asserts parity with the Rust resolution order.
close_reason: find_database_path() added to backends mirroring the Rust chain; CLI _get_controller uses it so 'crystal list' opens the real DB instead of DemoBackend. Controller default left unchanged to protect demo-mode tests. 5 hermetic tests; 496 passed.

title: [batch-submission-response-tracking] Track per-job results from batch submission instead of discarding request IDs
description:
submit_batch (src/app.rs:5041-5071) generates a request_id per job, fires request_submit_job, discards the result with `let _ =`, never stores the id, then immediately closes the modal and shows a generic status regardless of actual success/failure. The user cannot tell which batch jobs succeeded. Store each request_id in a batch-tracking map, update the JobSubmitted handler to mark per-job status in batch_submission.jobs[i], and close the modal only once all responses arrive or a timeout elapses.

AREA: Rust TUI
EFFORT: M | BLOCKER: False
KEY FILES: src/app.rs
DEPENDS ON (slugs): none
acceptance_criteria: Batch submission shows per-job submitted/failed status driven by actual JobSubmitted responses; the modal stays open until all responses arrive or it times out; covered by an app test.

title: [wire-template-browser-render] Make Template Browser Enter render the template into the editor
description:
Template Browser Enter (src/main.rs:1841-1848) has a TODO and just closes the modal without sending an RPC. The BridgeResponse::TemplateRendered handler (src/app.rs:1334-1341) only debug-logs the byte count and drops the content — request_render_template is wired but only invoked from test mocks. Verification downgraded P1->P2 (secondary feature; core D12/Materials path unaffected). Fix: on Enter call app.request_render_template(name, default_params); in the TemplateRendered Ok arm call self.open_file(filename, rendered) and switch to the Editor tab, mirroring the D12Generated handling at app.rs:1095-1098.

AREA: Rust TUI
EFFORT: M | BLOCKER: False
KEY FILES: src/main.rs, src/app.rs
DEPENDS ON (slugs): none
acceptance_criteria: Selecting a template and pressing Enter renders it via IPC and loads the result into the Editor tab; failures surface via set_error; covered by an app test using the mock bridge.

title: [unify-jsonrpc-dispatch-registries] Unify the two JSON-RPC dispatch registries (HANDLER_REGISTRY vs CrystalController._rpc_registry)
description:
There are two parallel dispatch namespaces. The Rust bridge sends legacy names (fetch_jobs, fetch_clusters, submit_job) that fall through to CrystalController.dispatch(), while server/handlers/*.py registers a dotted namespace (jobs.list, jobs.submit, clusters.list) checked first in server/__init__.py:260. app.rs also sends some dotted names (jobs.submit/jobs.status) via the untyped RpcResult path. The result is duplicate/overlapping methods (jobs.list, clusters.list, recipes.list registered in both, with different param handling and an unguarded JobStatus(status_str) ValueError in the handler), and orphaned handler modules. ADR-006 already flags this as a follow-up. Consolidate on one method-naming scheme end to end (Rust <-> server) and remove the duplicates; add try/except around JobStatus parsing.

AREA: crystalmath-server / dispatch
EFFORT: M | BLOCKER: False
KEY FILES: python/crystalmath/server/__init__.py, python/crystalmath/server/handlers/jobs.py, python/crystalmath/server/handlers/clusters.py, python/crystalmath/api.py, src/bridge.rs
DEPENDS ON (slugs): migrate-tui-imports-into-core
acceptance_criteria: Each RPC method is registered in exactly one place; Rust and Python agree on method names; invalid status strings return a structured error not an uncaught ValueError; an integration test exercises jobs.submit/status/cancel end to end.

title: [controller-init-error-surfacing] Surface controller/DB init failures instead of masking them as Method-not-found
description:
CrystalController init failures are swallowed (server/__init__.py:157-160 returns None on any exception). When controller is None and a method is not in HANDLER_REGISTRY, dispatch returns JSONRPC_METHOD_NOT_FOUND (line 277-280), which the Rust bridge maps to a generic 'Method not found' — the real init error (e.g. missing DB or tui import) never reaches the user. Separately, _init_sqlite's failure path sets _db=None silently, making all cluster/SLURM/job methods return NO_DATABASE. Add an eager startup health check that logs the real controller/DB init failure at WARN/ERROR, and return JSONRPC_INTERNAL_ERROR ('server not ready: <reason>') rather than METHOD_NOT_FOUND when the controller is None. Also replace asyncio.get_event_loop() at server/__init__.py:268 with get_running_loop() (deprecated in 3.10+).

AREA: crystalmath-server / dispatch
EFFORT: S | BLOCKER: False
KEY FILES: python/crystalmath/server/__init__.py, python/crystalmath/api.py
DEPENDS ON (slugs): migrate-tui-imports-into-core
acceptance_criteria: A failed controller/DB init produces a clear logged reason and an INTERNAL_ERROR (with cause) to the client rather than a misleading METHOD_NOT_FOUND; get_running_loop is used; covered by a dispatch test.

title: [store-spawned-server-child-handle] Retain spawned crystalmath-server Child handle for clean shutdown
description:
src/ipc/client.rs:235 binds the spawned server to `_child` which drops at end of ensure_server_running(), so the Rust side permanently loses the ability to SIGTERM/kill/reap the server on TUI exit. IpcBridgeHandle::drop only signals the Rust worker, never the Python child, so the server lingers up to 300s (its own inactivity timeout at server/__init__.py:391-409) after the TUI quits. Verification downgraded P1->P2 (inactivity timeout bounds the leak; not a crash risk). Fix: store the Child in IpcBridgeHandle (or a OnceLock<Mutex<Child>>) and call child.kill()+child.wait() from IpcBridgeHandle::drop. Replace the socket-file-existence poll in ensure_server_running (lines 239-251) with an actual UnixStream::connect retry loop to close the accept-readiness race.

AREA: IPC keystone
EFFORT: S | BLOCKER: False
KEY FILES: src/ipc/client.rs, src/bridge_ipc.rs, python/crystalmath/server/__init__.py
DEPENDS ON (slugs): none
acceptance_criteria: On TUI exit the spawned server is terminated promptly (not after 300s); ensure_server_running succeeds via real connect attempts rather than file-existence + fixed sleep; an integration test covers spawn+shutdown.
close_reason: Spawned servers retained in SPAWNED_SERVERS registry + shutdown_spawned_servers() called from IpcBridgeHandle::drop (kill+reap). Readiness check now uses real connect-retry instead of socket-file poll. Both clippy configs + fmt clean; IPC tests pass.

title: [validate-sql-column-distinct] Add column-name validation to jobflow_store.distinct() (SQL injection)
description:
python/crystalmath/integrations/jobflow_store.py:566 interpolates the caller-supplied `field` directly into `SELECT DISTINCT {field} FROM ...` with no validation, and the criteria keys at 572-573 also skip the `_VALID_COLUMN` regex that find() applies (lines 428-434/451-452). Verification confirmed but downgraded to P2: SQLite is a local file with no network attack surface and the only current caller passes a literal. Still violates AGENTS.md security rules and must be fixed before production. Fix: reuse the _VALID_COLUMN regex (promote it to module level) and validate both `field` and criteria keys, raising ValueError on invalid names. (_collection_name is already validated in connect() and is safe.)

AREA: Security
EFFORT: S | BLOCKER: False
KEY FILES: python/crystalmath/integrations/jobflow_store.py
DEPENDS ON (slugs): none
acceptance_criteria: distinct() raises ValueError on a non-identifier field or criteria key; a unit test covers an injection attempt; _VALID_COLUMN is applied consistently across find()/distinct()/remove_docs().
close_reason: distinct() now validates field + criteria keys via shared _validate_column/_VALID_COLUMN; query() consolidated onto the same helper. 6 new injection tests; 486 passed. (remove_docs is still a NotImplementedError stub; must validate when implemented.)

title: Generalize the calculation seam to CalculatorStage with MLIP/foundation calculators and content-addressed cache

Realizes ADR-021/022. Deep backlog: gated on the entire DFT spine being real.

title: [flaky] src/lsp.rs parallel tests race on CRYSTAL_NODE_PATH env var
description: Several src/lsp.rs tests (test_lsp_client_start_nonexistent_node_binary + siblings ~lsp.rs:1484/1733/1802) set/remove the process-global env var CRYSTAL_NODE_PATH concurrently. Under parallel scheduling one test's set_var('...nonexistent...') can be overwritten/removed by a sibling, so LspClient::start spawns successfully and the is_err() assertion fails (~1/run under load; passes serial with --test-threads=1). Pre-existing (src/lsp.rs unchanged in PR #10), surfaced by the Codex-fix workflow's stress runs. Fix: serialize the env-mutating lsp tests (a shared mutex or #[serial]) or pass the node path via a builder arg instead of process env.

title: [trim-llm-extra-and-ai-key-check] Trim unused langchain deps and add ANTHROPIC_API_KEY preflight check
description:
The [llm] extra (python/pyproject.toml:64-68) pulls langchain + langchain-anthropic (~200MB of transitive deps) but ai/service.py:7 only imports anthropic; grep shows zero langchain usage. Remove langchain/langchain-anthropic from [llm]. Separately, AIService constructs Anthropic(api_key=... or os.getenv('ANTHROPIC_API_KEY')) (service.py:53) which accepts None and fails late inside the first request, and check_ai_available_json (api.py:2110-2130) checks only the import, not the key. Add a key-presence check so the Rust AI tab can show 'unavailable: set ANTHROPIC_API_KEY' up front.

AREA: AI subsystem / packaging
EFFORT: S | BLOCKER: False
KEY FILES: python/pyproject.toml, python/crystalmath/ai/service.py, python/crystalmath/api.py
DEPENDS ON (slugs): none

close_reason: Removed unused langchain/langchain-anthropic from [llm]; AIService.__init__ fails fast on missing key. (check_ai_available_json already did the TUI-level key check - audit was partly stale.) Tests added. 491 passed.

title: [harden-ssh-known-hosts-and-quoting] Harden SSH host-key handling and shell quoting in the SLURM runner
description:
Defense-in-depth items in python/crystalmath/integrations/slurm_runner.py: _get_known_hosts returns an empty tuple () via type:ignore when known_hosts is absent (572-577) — asyncssh behavior for () is undocumented and may skip verification; return None (system defaults) and warn instead. allow_insecure has no production guard (152) — refuse to set it True when CRYSTALMATH_ENV=production. scancel/squeue interpolate slurm_job_id without shlex.quote (1547/1285) — inconsistent with the quoted remote_dir; add shlex.quote for consistency even though the id is regex-constrained to digits. (SSH host-key verification defaults to secure / allow_insecure=False, which verification confirmed is the correct posture.)

AREA: Security / SLURM
EFFORT: S | BLOCKER: False
KEY FILES: python/crystalmath/integrations/slurm_runner.py
DEPENDS ON (slugs): none
acceptance_criteria: Missing known_hosts yields system-default verification with a warning, not silent skip; allow_insecure cannot be enabled in production env; scancel/squeue use shlex.quote; covered by tests.
close_reason: Production guard on allow_insecure (__post_init__ + CRYSTALMATH_ENV); shlex.quote on squeue/sacct/scancel job ids; documented secure fail-closed known_hosts=() (corrected the audit's insecure None suggestion). 2 new tests; 491 passed.

title: [resolve-ipc-dead-code] Wire or gate the 9 dead-code warnings on the IPC build
description:
`cargo build --no-default-features` emits 9 dead-code warnings. Confirmed genuine: VaspInputState::set_status (src/ui/vasp_input.rs:230) is bypassed by direct field assignment in app.rs (2010/2021/4223) — replacing those assignments with set_status() is the correct fix since it also clears the paired error field. RecipeBrowserState::new (src/ui/recipes.rs:44) and WorkflowState::new (src/ui/workflows.rs:44) are trivial Default wrappers used only in #[cfg(test)] blocks — gate with #[cfg(test)] or remove and use ::default() in tests. Needed before `-D warnings` can be applied to the IPC clippy CI step and before the default-feature flip.

AREA: Rust TUI
EFFORT: S | BLOCKER: False
KEY FILES: src/ui/vasp_input.rs, src/ui/recipes.rs, src/ui/workflows.rs, src/app.rs
DEPENDS ON (slugs): none
acceptance_criteria: `cargo clippy --no-default-features -- -D warnings` passes with no dead-code warnings; set_status is used on the production validation path; no behavior change.
close_reason: 9 IPC dead-code warnings resolved (set_status wired; test-only new() cfg-gated; pending API #[allow(dead_code)]'d w/ rationale; Default impls added). Went further: cargo clippy -D warnings now clean on BOTH feature configs (autofixes + 2 checked_div). fmt clean; tests green.

title: Add the guarded MCP control plane, campaign/acquisition strategy, MLIP trust evaluation, and model registry
description: The top of the redesign stack — entirely greenfield, gated on the CalculatorStage/CAS layer. Add a CampaignController that composes (never bypasses) the make_*_flow factories and emits jobflow Response(detour/replace) for propose->MLIP-screen->DFT-validate->retrain loops, exposed to LLM agents via a guarded MCP tool-server over the stdio JSON-RPC with a closed verb set and TUI-gated approval (ADR-023; reframes the existing free-text ai/service.py). Add pluggable typed AcquisitionStrategy.score + CampaignStrategy owning budget/convergence/stopping with OOD-must-hit-DFT (ADR-025). Add an EvaluationHarness with per-property metrics on OOD splits, calibrated UncertaintyEstimate (deep-ensemble + conformal), and an applicability-domain/in_domain gate (ADR-026). Add a unified ModelIdentifier + navigable ModelRegistry/DatasetRegistry over the CAS (ADR-027). Realizes ADR-023/025/026/027.
